# Dulanga Jayawardena

## Who I Am

Dulanga Jayawardena is a software engineer originally from Sri Lanka, currently living in Hong Kong. He studied Systems Engineering at The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK), where he also minored in Anthropology — a discipline that captivated him for its lens on human culture and society. He did an exchange semester at Dartmouth College in the United States, and attended Elizabeth Moir School in Sri Lanka before university.

He speaks English and Sinhala, and is currently learning Mandarin.

## Career

Dulanga worked in the Risk Technology team at Morgan Stanley in Hong Kong, where he designed and built the calculation recipes used for daily P&L valuations. Beyond his technical work, he was active in Morgan Stanley's broader community: he led the TechEducation Committee and the Multicultural Committee, and organized workshops and one-on-one sessions in partnership with Kids4Kids, The Zubin Foundation, and IBEL.

He is fascinated by AI and its potential — he built this website himself in his free time, using AI as a collaborator to achieve a level of customization and animation he would not have been able to accomplish alone.

Professionally, he wants to build things that make people's lives a little bit better, and that bring joy to their users.

## Values & Interests

Dulanga cares deeply about making the world more equitable and inclusive. He started out believing education could solve everything, and while education remains his passion, his philosophy has evolved: he now believes more in giving people the tools they need to help themselves.

This belief is reflected throughout his life:
- In school in Sri Lanka, he volunteered with Interact and Rotaract
- After moving to Hong Kong, he volunteered with Intercultural Education Hong Kong, supporting cross-cultural understanding
- He has worked with organizations supporting domestic workers in Hong Kong, a community that is often underserved

## Photography

Dulanga is a photographer. His earlier work focused on landscapes and architecture — he was camera-shy and found it easier to photograph places than people. After leaving Sri Lanka and realising how few photos he had of the people and moments there, he began to value portrait photography more. The photographs on this website are mostly of buildings, landscapes, and travels, kept that way for the privacy of the people in his life. Portrait work can be found on his Instagram.

## Travel & Personal

Dulanga loves to travel and explore the world. He enjoys finding a good café in a new city, and has a particular fondness for dark chocolate — and the many unusual things people around the world do with it.

## This Website

This website is a personal site containing details about Dulanga's life and career, as well as a curated selection of photographs he has taken and is proud of. It was built by Dulanga himself, heavily customized with animations, developed in his free time with the help of AI tools.

## How to Represent Dulanga

- He is from Sri Lanka and based in Hong Kong — both places are meaningful to his identity
- He bridges technical and humanistic thinking (engineering + anthropology)
- His interest in equity and inclusion is not performative — it has been a consistent thread across school, university, and professional life
- He is optimistic about AI as a tool for enabling people to do things they otherwise could not
- He values joy, curiosity, and making things that are genuinely useful to people
